Mill Creek flooded from strong thunderstorms causing flooding in town of Moab.

4 inch limbs and smaller branches downed. Vendor tents blown away and porta potties blown down during Whittle the Wood event as reported on Social Media.
28 people were stranded on San Juan River where Lime Creek enters, and eventually rescued. Boats and most gear were lost to floodwaters. 9000+ cfs at this point at Mexican Hat.
Fast moving flood waters covered the road for a distance of approximately 540 meters, and 1 meter deep.

Cottonwood wash had major flood, surrounding house and filling flood plain. 20,000 cfs on San Juan River at Mexican Hat increased river level 10 feet, and loosened rafts with gear floating downstream.
Large tree blown down blocking Maple Street. Police Dept. reports 1 car smashed by tree as per social media post.
Colorado Highway 64 was closed due to flooding and mudslide around mile marker 65.
MTJ reported 70 mph wind gusts. Large tree branches down.
MTJ had a 70 mph wind gusts. Large tree limbs down.

UDOT reported mud and debris flowing over Hwy 191 at the entrance to Arches NP. The road is closed until further notice.
1.18 inches of rain fell in about a half an hour, causing flooding in the area. Floodwaters moved wood and rocks into nearby creek. Internet service was lost.

4 mudslides occurred between mile markers 72 and 77 along Highway 145. Slides estimated to have occurred between 1645 and 1700 MT. One potentially two bigger slides focused between mile markers 76 and 77. Highway 145 closed from mile marker 72 and 78 at 1710 MT.

Minor flooding reported in Indian Creek wash where it intersects with Park HWY 211 in Canyonlands National Park Needles District. Heavy rains fell upstream between 1800 - 1900 MT.

Flash flooding reported in Salt Creek wash where it intersects with Park HWY 211 in Canyonlands National Park Needles District. Water flowing rapidly in the wash with some small tree branches. Heavy rains fell upstream between 1730 - 1830 MT.

Heavy rain resulted in erosion on highway 141 bridge at MM 85.6 over Mesa Creek. CDOT closed hwy 141 on 8/12 in both directions at 11:10 AM MDT.

Multiple mudslides and flooding overnight in East Telluride, the Bridal Veil area, and Tomboy Road resulted in the following pass closures: from the East Spur at Town of Telluride entrance all the way east up to Black Bear is closed. Black Bear Pass is also closed. Tomboy Road which also experienced mudslides and flooding has Imogene Pass closed. Time based on radar.
